Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 7:

Ping for Review (Except Tim)

Patch Set 7:

I'll defer if we've changed course on these, but I don't like a "fix all the checkpatch issues" patch.  I think it's sprawling and a bit too hard to review.

I'd rather it broken down by category at least until it's smaller (I won't ask for tiny patches for every category)

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> I'll defer if we've changed course on these, but I don't like a "fix all the checkpatch issues" patch.  I think it's sprawling and a bit too hard to review.
> 
> I'd rather it broken down by category at least until it's smaller (I won't ask for tiny patches for every category)

This is mostly white space changes (Based on a quick test, over half of Lustre's remaining warnings are just white space). Breaking it down more I think would be too laborious.

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> I'll defer if we've changed course on these, but I don't like a "fix all the checkpatch issues" patch.  I think it's sprawling and a bit too hard to review.
> 
> I'd rather it broken down by category at least until it's smaller (I won't ask for tiny patches for every category)


Pat, valid concern. Sometimes checkpatch changes can be very large. This is one of the smaller ones. The changes are almost all "space/tab" related. (Removing spaces between variables, Moving 'ENTRY', moving "*/" to next line...). Only two changes which are not spaces related are added under 6142. First is adding where ever applicable "%s:... : %d\n" to CERROR etc... Second, Andreas also goes a step ahead and corrects the comments message as we go along the way. Making it more context relevant, fixing grammar etc... 

As, Tim mentioned. Breaking down more would be more work (For reviewers also). And I do not know where to draw a line to put it into next patch.

Please review :-)

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

While I agree it isn't as nice to have one larger patch doing the cleanup, there are finite resources for review and testing all of these patches, so for "no-op" patches like this I would prefer fewer rather than more. I think Arshad is doing a good job to balance the size of these patches.

Patch Set 7:

More than happy to defer on this one, then.  It is very nice to get these fixed and, yeah, since the WC process is not good at patch series, larger patches are appropriate for this sort of thing.  (A process that's really lightweight for series, like the kernel process, would push the balance the other way.  But that's not our situation.)
Patch Set 7: Code-Review+2
